summary: Matched the common aerial-landing action by replacing the ft_08A4.s assembly for ft_8008D5FC, ft_8008D708, and the ftCo_LandingAir callbacks with a new ftCo_LandingAir.c/.h. The PR also corrected related callback signatures to accept a float landing-lag parameter, renamed aerial landing-lag fields in ftCo_DatAttrs, tightened ftCommonData->xE4 to int, and moved prototypes out of the broad ft_0877.h into action-specific headers. The author noted that many signature changes were needed to make ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithLag match and expressed uncertainty about GET_FIGHTER codegen.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementation for ft_8008D5FC, ft_8008D708, ftCo_LandingAir_Anim, ftCo_LandingAir_IASA, ftCo_LandingAir_Phys, ftCo_LandingAir_Coll, and their local sdata2 constants from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_LandingAir.c to implement ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithLag, ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithMsidLag, and the LandingAir anim/IASA/phys/coll callbacks.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_LandingAir.h with address-commented declarations for the new matched functions.
Added ftCo_LandingAir.c.o to obj_files.mk so the new C translation unit participates in the text object build.
Changed ftCo_AttackAir_Coll and Peach float-attack collision paths to call ft_80082C74 with ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithLag instead of the raw ft_8008D5FC symbol.
Changed ft_80082C74's declared callback type from HSD_GObjEvent to void (*)(ftCo_GObj*, float lag), revealing that the callback receives a landing-lag argument.
Updated multiple air-to-ground or landing callback functions in Donkey, Game & Watch, Mewtwo, Peach, and Pikachu code to accept an extra float lag parameter, even where that parameter is unused.
Replaced raw ft_8008D708 calls in Game & Watch aerial landing init code with ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithMsidLag and renamed co_attrs field uses to landingair*_lag.

Renamed ftCo_DatAttrs aerial landing-lag fields from x1F8_NAirLandingLag/x1FC_FAirLandingLag/x200_BAirLandingLag/x204_UAirLandingLag/x208_DAirLandingLag to landingairn_lag, landingairf_lag, landingairb_lag, landingairhi_lag, and landingairlw_lag.
Changed ftCommonData field xE4 from float to int, matching its use as a threshold compared against fp->x67F.

Removed many common attack and LandingAir declarations from ft_0877.h and added direct includes such as ftCo_AttackAir.h, ftCo_AttackDash.h, ftCo_AttackHi3.h, ftCo_AttackHi4.h, ftCo_AttackLw3.h, ftCo_AttackLw4.h, ftCo_AttackS3.h, ftCo_AttackS4.h, and ftCo_LandingAir.h in ftCo_Init.c.
smart_moves: Identified that the ft_80082C74 collision helper is not a generic HSD_GObjEvent user: its callback has a second float lag parameter. Propagating that signature through callers made ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithLag match.

Split the generic landing-air entry into two named helpers: ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithLag for the attack-air-specific dispatch and ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithMsidLag for direct motion-state-plus-lag entry. This preserved reuse for character-specific landing variants such as Game & Watch.
Kept game quirks intact while improving names: Game & Watch's up-air landing still intentionally uses Back Aerial landing lag, now documented with a clearer comment and landingairb_lag field name.
Used action-specific headers instead of continuing to grow ft_0877.h, reducing dependency opacity and making the matched code easier to include where needed.
Inserted MUST_MATCH-only unused stack arrays in ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithLag and ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithMsidLag, indicating stack-shape preservation was necessary for the match.
Renamed data fields only where behavior in the matched code made their meaning clear, especially the five co_attrs aerial landing-lag slots.
decomp_lessons: When a callback match is stubborn, inspect the caller ABI: a function pointer typed as a generic event may actually pass extra arguments, and downstream function signatures may need to be widened even if the new parameter is unused.
Landing-air logic maps current aerial attack motion IDs to corresponding landing-air motion IDs and per-aerial lag attributes; this pattern can guide future aerial landing decompilation.
Preserve apparent game bugs during cleanup. The diff keeps Game & Watch LandingAirHi using landingairb_lag, with a comment noting the erroneous Back Aerial lag use.
Struct field type corrections can be match-critical: p_ftCommonData->xE4 is compared as an integer threshold against fp->x67F, so declaring it int rather than float better matches the original code.
Header cleanup can be part of a matching PR when the match depends on accurate prototypes; moving declarations from broad numeric headers to per-action headers improves type information.

Be cautious with GET_FIGHTER. The PR body says the author was starting to suspect GET_FIGHTER is fake, and several changes replace GET_FIGHTER(gobj) with gobj->user_data under a @todo #GET_FIGHTER note to influence matching.
naming_conventions: Matched common action files use ftCo_<Action>.c and ftCo_<Action>.h, here ftCo_LandingAir.c and ftCo_LandingAir.h.
Entry helpers were named by purpose and parameters: ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithLag and ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithMsidLag.
Common landing-air callbacks follow ftCo_LandingAir_Anim, ftCo_LandingAir_IASA, ftCo_LandingAir_Phys, and ftCo_LandingAir_Coll.
Aerial landing-lag attributes were renamed to lowercase semantic names with the aerial direction embedded: landingairn_lag, landingairf_lag, landingairb_lag, landingairhi_lag, landingairlw_lag.
Header declarations retained address comments such as /* 08D5FC */ and /* 08D708 */ for searchability and symbol mapping.
Temporary uncertainty around GET_FIGHTER was marked explicitly with /// @todo #GET_FIGHTER rather than hidden.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a 164-line assembly block in ft_08A4.s with C and removed now-unneeded local constants .L_804D84B8, .L_804D84C0, .L_804D84C4, and .L_804D84C8.
Used MUST_MATCH conditional unused stack buffers, u8 _[20] and u8 _[8], to reproduce original stack layout without affecting non-matching builds.
Matched the landing-lag reduction path by computing div_lag = lag / p_ftCommonData->xE8, converting to int, clamping zero to one, and assigning the integer back to lag.
Called Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, msid, Ft_MF_None, NULL, 0, 1, 0), then set animation rate to (ftAnim_8006F484(gobj) + 0.1f) / lag, matching the old ft_8008D708 behavior.
Adjusted function-pointer declarations and many target functions to accept float lag, avoiding casts or incompatible generic callback typing.
Used direct gobj->user_data in some functions with a GET_FIGHTER todo, suggesting macro expansion or aliasing differences affected code generation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The only explicit rationale comes from the PR body: many function signatures had to change to get ftCo_LandingAir_EnterWithLag to match, and the author was unsure whether GET_FIGHTER accurately represents original codegen.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for #GET_FIGHTER or gobj->user_data to see whether the GET_FIGHTER macro concern was resolved.
Find subsequent changes to ft_80082C74 to confirm its final name and callback typedef.
Search for remaining direct references to ft_8008D5FC or ft_8008D708 after this PR.
Review later ftCo_DatAttrs cleanup for other x1F8-style field names converted to semantic landing or action attributes.
Check whether Game & Watch LandingAirHi's intentional landingairb_lag quirk was preserved in later refactors.
